US: Muslim woman sues sheriff’s office over forced removal of hijab
US: Muslim woman sues sheriff’s office over forced removal of hijab

comments.body: Is it curiosity to see what's under the hijab that makes them so disrespectful and malicious? At least the Muslim women win lawsuits so the next 'law' enforcement officer who is tempted to take off hijabs or share pix will think twice especially if their pay is docked.

People are typically required to remove headwear such as hats for mugshots. Mugshots are routinely disseminated to the public. No, it’s not likely that the police care to see the hair under some random woman’s hijab - hair is usually considered quite normal in this country and not something sexually exciting which is to be kept hidden. To most Americans, revealing one’s hair has no significance whatsoever.

That's the first misconception about hijab that hair is covered because it can be sexually exciting. Beautiful hair on beautiful women may be but for the ordinary Muslim women, hijab is worn out of modesty in an effort to fulfil one aspect of faith. Many Muslim women don't wear hijab and they are still practicing Muslims. Nuns who dress modestly choose to do so for the same reason, out of faith.

The Muslim woman in the news item yook off her hijab for the mugshot and the police stated thst this photo will be on file and not disseminated. The fact that is was uploaded and shared made it offensive the same way if a nun had to remove her head covering and her picture was uploaded to the public. It boils down to a matter of respect.
